Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Canada Transportation Act (passenger rail service)
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Canada Transportation Act (passenger rail service)

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Canada Transportation Act to require that railway companies give priority to passenger rail service over the movement of freight and to provide for administrative monetary penalties for contraventions of this requirement.
His Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Rail Passenger Priority Act.
The Canada Transportation Act is amended by adding the following after section 139:

If there is any conflict in respect of the operation of a railway line between the movement of traffic and the movement of passengers, railway companies must give priority to passenger rail service on that railway line.
Section 177 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2.1):

Subsection.Text: The contravention of section 139.1 may be proceeded with as a violation in accordance with sections 179 and 180. The maximum amount payable for each violation is $250,000.

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 178(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Agency, in respect of a violation referred to in subsection 177(1), (2.1) or (3), or the Minister, in respect of a violation referred to in subsection 177(2), (2.001), (2.01), (2.11) or (2.2), may

Section.Text: Subsection 180.8(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: In the case of a violation referred to in subsection 177(2), (2.001), (2.01), (2.11) or (2.2), the Minister may delegate to the Agency any power, duty or function conferred on the Minister under this Part.
